title: Replace storage eprintln! diagnostics with typed warnings
description:
Replace storage-layer `eprintln!` diagnostics with typed warnings propagated to the output layer (report boundary leak). Storage must not write to stderr directly.

## Sites
- `storage/claim_coordinator.rs:963,1052,1066`, plus `storage/lock_cleanup.rs` and `storage/worktree_identity.rs` — currently emit `eprintln!` diagnostics inline.

## Approach
Propagate a typed `Vec<StorageWarning>` (e.g. `SequenceGap`, `IndexRebuilt`, `TempCleanupFailed`) up to the output layer, or route via `tracing::warn!`. Choose one mechanism consistently with how the rest of the crate surfaces non-fatal diagnostics.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] No `eprintln!`/`println!` for diagnostics remains in `storage/`; non-fatal storage conditions are represented as typed warnings (`StorageWarning` enum) or routed through the project's logging facility.
- [ ] The output layer (not storage) decides how/whether warnings are rendered to the user; `--json` callers are unaffected by stray stderr writes from storage.
- [ ] Each previously-printed condition still surfaces (covered by a test asserting the warning is produced).
- [ ] `cargo-ci` and `code-review` pass.
